entries.source:
Chat Edit: 'File Operations (9 features) - Dialogs, recent files, save/load
Menu Command System (25 features) - Centralized routing'

Chat Edit: 'Quick update: I attempted a rebuild to validate the floating panel fix and hit several compile/link issues. I’ve patched the immediate errors and summarized what’s left to make it build cleanly.

What I’ve done

Fixed floating panel stubs:
Resolved undefined panelWidth/panelHeight by computing client size before creating the status label.
Removed duplicated switch/WinMain code in Win32IDE_P3_Stubs.cpp that caused brace/scope errors.
Added required includes:
Win32IDE_FileOps.cpp: #include <commctrl.h> and #include <algorithm> for SB_SETTEXT and std::find.
Win32IDE_Commands.cpp: #include <commctrl.h> and #include <richedit.h> for status bar and rich edit APIs.
Current blockers

Duplicate symbols: Win32IDE::openFile() is defined in both Win32IDE_FileOps.cpp and Win32IDE_clean.cpp. We need a single authoritative implementation to avoid multiple definition errors.
Entry point missing: Linker complains about undefined WinMain. It looks like the actual WinMain lives in a different file than the set I compiled. We should include the proper app entry file in the build list (or ensure Win32IDE_clean.cpp provides the entry).
Proposed next steps

Identify the correct entry file:
Which file is intended to define WinMain for this target? If it’s Win32IDE_clean.cpp, I’ll adjust and include it properly. Otherwise, add the right file to the build command.
Resolve duplicate implementations:
Confirm whether openFile() and related file ops should live only in Win32IDE_FileOps.cpp. If so, I’ll remove/disable overlapping implementations in Win32IDE_clean.cpp.
Re-run the build:
Once the above are aligned, I’ll compile and verify runtime behavior for:
Floating Panel toggle (IDM_VIEW_FLOATING_PANEL)
File dialogs and recent files
Centralized command routing
Would you like me to:

Search the workspace for WinMain and the duplicate openFile definitions and then harmonize them?
Or share your intended entry file and target file list, so I can update the build command accordingly?
For reference, I’m using GPT-5.'

Chat Edit: '2. Title Bar (Top Center/Right)
Displays the name of the currently open file and project folder.
Window Controls: Standard minimize, maximize/restore, and close buttons (on Windows/Linux).
Account/Settings Icons (Right): Icons may appear for your GitHub/Microsoft account and the Settings gear icon.'
